        targetUser
      • targetUser(i: Interaction): Option[User]

Payload extraction: typed slash options, resolved entities, context-menu targets, modal fields, and custom_id pattern parsing/matching.

The opt/req getters are what the slash macro's generated code calls; they are exported for programmatic handlers too.

Imports

types

Types

LeafOptions = Table[string, ApplicationCommandInteractionDataOption]

Consts

SelectKinds = {mctSelectMenu, mctUserSelect, mctRoleSelect,
               mctMentionableSelect, mctChannelSelect}

Procs

proc focusedOption(options: LeafOptions): Option[
    ApplicationCommandInteractionDataOption] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hasCaptures(pattern: CustomIdPattern): bool {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
proc leafOptions(data: ApplicationCommandInteractionData): tuple[
    path: seq[string], options: LeafOptions] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Descends acotSubCommandGroup/acotSubCommand nesting and returns the group/subcommand names walked plus the leaf table holding actual values. 
proc literalPrefixLen(pattern: CustomIdPattern): int {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
proc matchCustomId(pattern: CustomIdPattern; id: string): Option[
    Table[string, string]] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Matches an incoming custom_id against a pattern. Captures are non-greedy (end at the first occurrence of the following literal) and must be non-empty; int captures must parse as an integer. 
proc modalFields(components: seq[MessageComponent]): Table[string, string] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc opt(ctx: SlashContext; name: string; T: typedesc[Attachment]): Option[
    Attachment]
proc opt(ctx: SlashContext; name: string; T: typedesc[bool]): Option[bool]
proc opt(ctx: SlashContext; name: string; T: typedesc[float]): Option[float]
proc opt(ctx: SlashContext; name: string; T: typedesc[int]): Option[int]
proc opt(ctx: SlashContext; name: string; T: typedesc[Member]): Option[Member]
Guild-only: resolves the member and backfills member.user from the resolved users table (Discord omits it inside resolved.members). 
proc opt(ctx: SlashContext; name: string; T: typedesc[Mentionable]): Option[
    Mentionable]
proc opt(ctx: SlashContext; name: string; T: typedesc[ResolvedChannel]): Option[
    ResolvedChannel]
proc opt(ctx: SlashContext; name: string; T: typedesc[Role]): Option[Role]
proc opt(ctx: SlashContext; name: string; T: typedesc[string]): Option[string]
proc opt(ctx: SlashContext; name: string; T: typedesc[User]): Option[User]
proc parseCustomIdPattern(raw: string): CustomIdPattern {.
    ...raises: [DimslashError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Parses "page:{n:int}"-style patterns. Captures are {name} (string) or {name:int}; two captures must be separated by literal text. Raises DimslashError on malformed patterns (the DSL macros run this at compile time, turning these into compile errors). 
proc req[T](ctx: SlashContext; name: string; t: typedesc[T]): T
Required-option variant: raises DimslashError when the option is missing or unresolvable. Discord enforces required options client-side, so this only fires on malformed payloads. 
proc stringValue(opt: ApplicationCommandInteractionDataOption): Option[string] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
The option's value as text, regardless of underlying type. 
proc targetMember(i: Interaction): Option[Member] {....raises: [KeyError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc targetMessage(i: Interaction): Option[Message] {....raises: [KeyError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc targetUser(i: Interaction): Option[User] {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
